enonce.texte:
Match each disease with its correct symptom. Draw a line to connect them.

Diseases: Malaria, Cold, Cough, Headache, Stomachache

Symptoms: A. high fever and chills, B. runny nose and sneezing, C. pain in the head, D. pain in the belly, E. persistent throat irritation

questions.texte: Match each disease with its symptom.
questions.reponse:
1. Malaria → A. high fever and chills
2. Cold → B. runny nose and sneezing
3. Cough → E. persistent throat irritation
4. Headache → C. pain in the head
5. Stomachache → D. pain in the belly

sousQuestions.texte: Malaria → ______
Cough → ______
Headache → ______
Stomachache → ______

enonce.texte: Read the sentences and identify which part of the body is affected by each symptom.

questions.texte: Identify the body part for each symptom.
questions.reponse:
1. Headache → head
2. Stomachache → stomach
3. Sore throat → throat
4. Toothache → tooth
5. Earache → ear

sousQuestions.texte: I have a headache. Which part of my body hurts?
I have a stomachache. Which part of my body hurts?
I have a sore throat. Which part of my body hurts?
I have a toothache. Which part of my body hurts?
I have an earache. Which part of my body hurts?

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

Marie is Sick

Marie is a student in Form 6. She is not feeling well today. She has a high fever and a terrible headache. Her mother is very worried. Marie also has a cough and a runny nose. Her mother says, "You have a cold, Marie. You need to rest and drink a lot of water. I will call the doctor."

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the text.
questions.reponse:
1. Marie is a student in Form 6.
2. She has a high fever, a terrible headache, a cough, and a runny nose.
3. Her mother says she has a cold.

sousQuestions.texte: What class is Marie in?
List three symptoms Marie has.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. high fever 2. terrible headache 3. cough (or runny nose)

sousQuestions.texte: What does Marie's mother say Marie has?

enonce.texte: Unscramble the letters to form the correct disease or symptom vocabulary word.

sousQuestions.texte: RFEVE → ______
UGHCO → ______
ACDHHEAE → ______
MALRIA → ______
RRAEDIAH → ______

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

Protect Yourself from Malaria

Malaria is a very common disease in Cameroon. It is caused by mosquito bites. The symptoms of malaria include high fever, headache, and chills. Sometimes people also feel very tired and have muscle pain. To prevent malaria, you should sleep under a mosquito net. You should also wear long sleeves and use insect repellent. If you have a fever, you should go to the hospital quickly.

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the text.
questions.reponse:
1. Malaria is caused by mosquito bites.
2. Three symptoms of malaria: high fever, headache, chills (or tiredness, muscle pain).
3. To prevent malaria, you should sleep under a mosquito net, wear long sleeves, and use insect repellent.
4. You should go to the hospital quickly if you have a fever.

sousQuestions.texte: What causes malaria?
sousQuestions.reponse: Malaria is caused by mosquito bites.

sousQuestions.texte: List three symptoms of malaria mentioned in the text.
How can you prevent malaria? Give two ways.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Sleep under a mosquito net. 2. Wear long sleeves.

sousQuestions.texte: What should you do if you have a fever?
sousQuestions.reponse: You should go to the hospital quickly.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct disease or symptom vocabulary from the box.

Word Box: malaria, fever, headache, cough, stomachache, cold, diarrhea

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct word.

sousQuestions.texte: I have a terrible ______. I need to take some medicine.
She has a high ______. She is very hot.
He is sneezing and has a runny nose. He has a ______.
I ate too much and now I have a ______.
______ is caused by mosquito bites.

enonce.texte:
Your friend is sick and cannot come to school. Write a short paragraph (4-5 sentences) describing your friend's illness to your teacher. Include the symptoms and what your friend should do to get better.

Useful vocabulary: sick, fever, headache, cough, rest, drink water, doctor, medicine, hospital

questions.texte: Write a paragraph describing your friend's illness.
questions.reponse:
A good paragraph should include:
- The name of the friend and their condition
- At least 2-3 symptoms (fever, headache, cough, etc.)
- What the friend should do (rest, see a doctor, take medicine)
- Correct grammar and spelling
- 4-5 sentences

intitule: Dialogue Completion: At the Doctor's Office

enonce.texte:
Complete the dialogue between a doctor and a patient. Use the words from the box.

Word Box: headache, fever, cough, medicine, rest, water, throat

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct words.
questions.reponse:
1. cough
2. throat
3. fever
4. headache
5. medicine
6. rest
7. water

sousQuestions.texte:
Doctor: Good morning. What is wrong with you?
Patient: I have a bad ______.
Doctor: Does your ______ hurt?
Patient: Yes. I also have a ______ and a ______.
Doctor: You have a cold. Take this ______ and ______ at home. Drink lots of ______.

enonce.texte:
Read the situation and answer the questions.

Situation: In your school, many students are getting sick with malaria. The head teacher wants to help prevent this problem.

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the situation.
questions.reponse:
1. Malaria is spread by mosquito bites.
2. Students can help by sleeping under mosquito nets, wearing long sleeves, using insect repellent, and keeping the school environment clean.
3. Symptoms of malaria include high fever, headache, chills, tiredness, and muscle pain.

sousQuestions.texte: How is malaria spread?
sousQuestions.reponse: Malaria is spread by mosquito bites.

sousQuestions.texte: What can students do to prevent malaria at school? Give two suggestions.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Sleep under mosquito nets. 2. Keep the school environment clean to avoid mosquito breeding.

sousQuestions.texte: What are the symptoms of malaria? Name three.

intitule: Writing: A Letter to a Sick Friend

enonce.texte:
Your best friend is sick and cannot come to school. Write a short letter (5-6 sentences) to your friend. In your letter:

  • Ask how your friend is feeling.
  • Ask about their symptoms.
  • Give advice on what they should do to get better.
  • Say that you miss them and hope they get well soon.

questions.texte: Write a letter to your sick friend.
questions.reponse:
A good letter should include:
- A salutation (Dear [friend's name],)
- Questions about how they are feeling
- Questions about their symptoms
- Advice (rest, drink water, take medicine, see a doctor)
- A closing (I miss you. I hope you get well soon. / Your friend, [name])
